seoTitle: How to Customize Card Colors in Jira Boards
metaDescription: Learn how to change Jira board card colors, set up JQL-based color rules, and highlight priorities or issue types for better visibility.
intro: A board full of same-colored cards makes it harder to spot priorities at a glance. This guide covers customizing card colors on Jira boards, setting up JQL-based rules, and using color to highlight the things that matter most.

steps.heading: Open board settings
steps.body: Navigate to your board's settings to access the card color configuration options.

steps.heading: Configure card colors
steps.body: Choose the basis for coloring cards, such as issue type, priority, or assignee, from the available options.

steps.heading: Create JQL color rules
steps.body: Set up custom JQL queries to apply specific colors to cards that match conditions not covered by the default options.

steps.heading: Test and apply changes
steps.body: Save your settings and check the board to confirm the colors are highlighting the right cards as expected.

faq.question: How do I change card colors on a Jira board?
faq.answer: Open board settings, find the card color configuration, and choose a coloring basis like priority or issue type.

faq.question: Can I use JQL to set custom card colors?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira supports JQL-based color rules so you can highlight cards that match specific custom conditions.

faq.question: Does card color customization work on Kanban boards too?
faq.answer: Yes, card color settings are available on both Scrum and Kanban boards through board settings.

faq.question: Why would I customize card colors on a Jira board?
faq.answer: Color coding makes it faster to spot high-priority items, specific issue types, or particular assignees at a glance.
